qt: These men, along with their allies in Oklahoma's legislature, have committed to not hearing SB13, sentencing to death the roughly 5,000 babies who will be murdered in the state this year.

q: Why does the author argue Lauinger's stated objection to SB13 is suspect?
a: Lauinger opposes the bill because it would repeal the pro-life regulations he has helped pass, which the author calls either gross incompetence or proof that Lauinger prioritizes his own legislative legacy over actually ending abortion.
t: The Three Pro-Life Men Keeping Abortion Legal in Oklahoma
qt: In doing so, Lauinger either outed himself as the densest lobbyist in the country, not understanding that to end abortion, you have to get rid of regulations that allow abortion, or he is admitting that his priority is not ending abortion but maintaining his own legislative victories. There is simply no other explanation for his opposition.

q: How does the article rebut Treat's claim that passing SB13 could 'hurt the pro-life movement' through severability?
a: The author points out that line 19 of page three of SB13 explicitly makes the bill inseverable, so a court cannot strike down the abolition portions while preserving the regulatory repeals. He concludes Lauinger and Treat either haven't read the bill or are lying.
t: The Three Pro-Life Men Keeping Abortion Legal in Oklahoma
qt: Line 19 of page three makes SB13 inseverable. Lauinger and Treat either haven't read the bill or aren't being truthful.

q: What does Senator Joseph Silk say about his colleagues' constitutional reasoning?
a: Silk, the bill's author, says the senators opposing SB13 have no understanding of the Constitution and treat the courts as omnipotent, while also failing to do what they campaigned on.
t: The Three Pro-Life Men Keeping Abortion Legal in Oklahoma
qt: The Senators who oppose SB 13 have zero knowledge of the Constitution and how our republic is supposed to work. They believe that the courts are all powerful and can decide whatever they like whether it is constitutional or not.  They also have no intention of doing what they campaigned on and actually protect innocent life.

q: What does the author argue is the proper response when the Supreme Court legalizes murder?
a: Silberman insists that legislators' oath is to the Constitution, not the Supreme Court, and that the only acceptable response to Roe is to defy the court outright.
t: The Three Pro-Life Men Keeping Abortion Legal in Oklahoma
qt: When the Supreme Court so egregiously and intentionally violates the Constitution as to legalize murder, the *only* appropriate response is to [tell the court to pound sand](https://freethestates.org/nullification-and-interposition/index.html).

q: Does Silk believe SB13 would actually pass if it got a vote?
a: Yes. Silk says the Oklahoma Senate is overwhelmingly pro-life Republican in public statements and votes, and that the bill would pass a committee or full Senate vote, but Smalley and Treat are blocking it to avoid controversy.
t: The Three Pro-Life Men Keeping Abortion Legal in Oklahoma
qt: If SB 13 came to a committee or full senate vote it would pass with a majority. The Oklahoma Senate is overwhelmingly pro-life Republicans in public and votes, however [Smalley and Treat] will do whatever they can to keep it from coming to a vote because they don't want to [do] something that is controversial and impactful.

q: What political action does the author urge against Smalley and Treat?
a: Silberman calls for them to be primaried by candidates with the courage to defy the Supreme Court and protect unborn Oklahomans.
t: The Three Pro-Life Men Keeping Abortion Legal in Oklahoma
qt: If Smalley and Treat subject their constituents to the tyranny of the Supreme Court and Oklahoma's babies to the abortionist's knife, they should be primaried by people who aren't cowards.

q: How does the article characterize the broader posture of the fifty states toward the Constitution?
a: He claims every state in the union is presently in defiance of the Constitution by tolerating abortion, which means legislators who know this are mocking their oath of office and rejecting God.
t: The Three Pro-Life Men Keeping Abortion Legal in Oklahoma
qt: Indubitably, all fifty states are presently in defiance of the United States Constitution. If Smalley and Treat have read the Constitution, they know this. By allowing abortion, they mock their oath of office and rebuff their Creator.

q: What bill was killed in the Oklahoma Senate HHS committee on Feb. 3, 2021, and what would it have done?
a: Senator Warren Hamilton presented SB495, the Equal Protection and Equal Justice Act, which would have criminalized abortion as murder and extended equal protection to all preborn human beings in Oklahoma. All ten committee members voted against it.
t: Though they intended evil against You and devised a plot, they will not succeed.
qt: Oklahoma State Senator Warren Hamilton of District 7 presented the Equal Protection and Equal Justice Act (SB495) to the Senate Health and Human Services committee. This bill would’ve criminalized abortion as murder and provided equal protection to all preborn human beings in the state of Oklahoma.

q: How did the seven Republicans on the committee vote, and why does Hoffman find that significant?
a: All seven Republicans, alongside the three Democrats, voted no. Hoffman highlights this to puncture the assumption that pro-life Republican majorities will reliably advance abolition legislation.
t: Though they intended evil against You and devised a plot, they will not succeed.
qt: You might think, “Well of course this is going to pass! Seven pro-life Republicans will vote yes and it’ll pass!” Sadly, you would be wrong. Every single one of the senators listed above voted against the bill.

q: What happened to SB13, the nearly identical bill from 2020?
a: SB13 was filed by former Senator Joseph Silk and ignored by the same committee under chairman Greg McCortney, who refused to grant it a hearing. It died without ever being heard.
t: Though they intended evil against You and devised a plot, they will not succeed.
qt: In Feb. 2020, a nearly identical bill, Senate Bill 13, the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act filed by former State Senator Joseph Silk, was simply ignored by the very same committee. It died without ever even being heard. Republican pro-life Senator Greg McCortney, chairman of the Health and Human Services Committee, refused to allow the bill a hearing.

q: What does Hoffman allege about the role of Senate leadership in suppressing abolition bills?
a: He alleges that Greg Treat and Kim David, the most powerful pro-life Republicans in the chamber, have used backroom dealings and pressure on rank-and-file Republicans to stifle abolition legislation, and that Kim David personally whipped votes against SB13 on the Senate floor.
t: Though they intended evil against You and devised a plot, they will not succeed.
qt: It is very apparent that Senate leadership is doing everything in their power to stifle this bill and the Abolitionist Movement by means of backroom dealings and pressuring the rest of the Republican Senators.

q: What consequence did Joseph Silk reportedly face for advocating abolition?
a: Silk was stripped of his vice chairmanship in 2019 because of his advocacy for abolition, illustrating the cost imposed on legislators who break from leadership on this issue.
t: Though they intended evil against You and devised a plot, they will not succeed.
qt: Joseph Silk was stripped of his vice chairmanship in 2019 for his advocacy for abolition.

q: Why does Hoffman say the bill was killed so swiftly the week before Abolition Day?
a: He argues leadership timed the vote deliberately to deflate thousands of Christians planning to lobby at the Capitol, attempting to dampen morale rather than face sustained pressure.
t: Though they intended evil against You and devised a plot, they will not succeed.
qt: Killing the bill this swiftly, the week before Abolition Day when they know that thousands of Christians will be showing up to lobby for the bill, is clearly an attempt to dampen the spirits of those seeking to establish justice for the preborn in the state of Oklahoma.

q: How does Hoffman use Luke 18's parable of the Pharisee and tax collector to confront Oklahoma Christians?
a: He recasts the Pharisee's prayer as Oklahoma's self-congratulation about being more righteous than states like California or New York, then urges readers instead to take the tax collector's posture of humble repentance.
t: Though they intended evil against You and devised a plot, they will not succeed.
qt: “God, I thank you that we are not like other States. We are not like the corrupt, unjust, states like California and New York. We vote for political leaders who believe in God’s Word and establish justice accordingly. We are conservative, Republican, pro-life, and would never oppose a law to protect our preborn neighbors.”

q: What share of guilt does Hoffman assign to ordinary Oklahomans?
a: He says voters bear immense guilt for electing, supporting, and funding leaders who proved unworthy of the responsibility entrusted to them, calling for self-examination rather than blame shifted entirely onto politicians.
t: Though they intended evil against You and devised a plot, they will not succeed.
qt: It is also just as clearly evident that WE, the people of Oklahoma, bear an immense guilt for having elected these leaders, supported them, voted for them, and given money to their campaign efforts, believing them to be worthy of this responsibility when they are not.

q: What scriptural basis does Hoffman cite for publicly exposing the committee's actions?
a: He cites Ephesians 5:11 and Luke 12:1-3, arguing Christians must not merely abstain from unfruitful works of darkness but actively expose them, since whatever is whispered in private will be proclaimed on the housetops.
t: Though they intended evil against You and devised a plot, they will not succeed.
qt: Not only are we to take no part in the unfruitful works of darkness, but we are to instead expose them (Eph 5:11). This shameful, sinful action taken by the members of the Health and Human Services committee must be exposed.

q: What concrete action does Hoffman recommend regarding existing bills filed under titles 12, 21, and 63?
a: He urges constituents to ask their House Rep and Senator whether they have any bill, in committee or already passed out of committee, under titles 12, 21, or 63, and to request the language be replaced with the exact text of SB495.
t: Though they intended evil against You and devised a plot, they will not succeed.
qt: Senate Bill 495 was filed under title 12, 21, and 63. Ask your House Rep and State Senator if they have a bill, either in committee or even one that has already passed in committee with any of the titles 12, 21, or 63, and request they completely change the language in the bill to the exact language that was in Senate Bill 495.

q: Why does Hoffman insist the Abolition Day rally must be large despite the SB495 vote?
a: Because the legislature gauges crowd size to decide whether passing a bill is politically necessary, a massive turnout signals that opposing abolition will cost re-election, countering leadership's plan to demoralize abolitionists.
t: Though they intended evil against You and devised a plot, they will not succeed.
qt: This rally is extremely important, because the legislature evaluates the size of the crowd to determine whether it is important for them to pass our bill or not, and how likely they are to lose re-election if they oppose the abolition bill.

q: What does Hoffman argue about the authority of Christ over civil government?
a: He argues Christ in the Great Commission claimed all authority in heaven and on earth, so Oklahoma's state leaders who refuse to abolish abortion are living in active rebellion against the Lord Jesus Christ.
t: Though they intended evil against You and devised a plot, they will not succeed.
qt: The Lord Jesus declared victory, power, and rule,  possessing ALL authority in heaven AND on earth, and as such, our state leaders are living in rebellion against the authority of the Lord Jesus Christ.

q: What first sparked Timothy Cole's lifelong concern about grave societal evil?
a: Learning in junior high history class about American slavery, the mistreatment of Native Americans, the Civil War, and the civil rights era shattered his assumption that grave evil was confined to ancient times and motivated him to fight the evils of his own age.
t: Timothy Cole: My Conversion From Pro-Life Speaker to Abolitionist
qt: Learning of our nation’s history of slavery, mistreatment of Native Americans, the Civil War, the civil rights era etc. disturbed me to my core and removed my imagined barrier of time between then and now, taking some of my innocence.

q: What pro-life paradigm did Cole encounter while serving on a pregnancy care center board?
a: He was taught that women are the 'second victim' of abortion, that pro-life laws exempt mothers from prosecution, and that abortion is openly discussed with mothers at the center 'because it is an option.'
t: Timothy Cole: My Conversion From Pro-Life Speaker to Abolitionist
qt: For example: women are the “second victim” of abortion, pro-life laws exempt women from prosecution, and abortion is an option discussed with mothers at pregnancy care centers “because it is an option.”

q: What moment at the National Right to Life conference in Overland Park first jolted Cole?
a: A speaker openly endorsed legislation containing rape, incest, and life-of-the-mother exceptions, which Cole understood as supporting the murder of certain babies; he left the workshop confused and in tears.
t: Timothy Cole: My Conversion From Pro-Life Speaker to Abolitionist
qt: I nearly had to pick my jaw up off the floor when a speaker said they support legislation with exceptions that allow for the murder of babies conceived in rape, incest and for danger to the life of the mother.

q: Why did Cole stay in the pro-life establishment despite his misgivings?
a: He was afraid of losing the only political outlet he knew of for fighting abortion and was persuaded by appeals to political realities and the supposed need to change hearts and minds before demanding justice.
t: Timothy Cole: My Conversion From Pro-Life Speaker to Abolitionist
qt: Probably out of a desire to not lose the only anti-abortion movement I was aware of, I too easily was talked back into the Pro-Life Establishment by assertions that we must do what we can given political realities, and that we must change hearts and minds before we can demand justice for the preborn.

q: What did Kansans For Life trainers tell Cole about their legislative strategy?
a: Trainers told him immediate ending of abortion was impossible, so the focus had to be on restricting abortion however possible, and that even failed pro-life measures were crafted to frame the debate and shift public opinion.
t: Timothy Cole: My Conversion From Pro-Life Speaker to Abolitionist
qt: I was told that even if the pro-life measures do not pass, they are crafted to frame the debate in a way that sways public opinion toward pro-life thinking.

q: How did a fellow Christian react to Kevan Myers' uncompromising no-abortion position?
a: The brother repeatedly warned Cole that Kevan was 'really extreme' for opposing all abortion, leaving Cole baffled that any serious Christian would consider 'no abortion' to be an extreme stance.
t: Timothy Cole: My Conversion From Pro-Life Speaker to Abolitionist
qt: “Kevan is really extreme on abortion… He is for no abortion… I mean he is for no abortion, NO abortion!”

q: What did Cole find missing from the Kansans For Life Value Them Both presentation?
a: The slides focused on parental notification, pre-abortion counseling, informed consent, and ultrasound access before a baby is killed, not on actually ending abortion, prompting Cole to shout that the presentation had nothing to do with ending abortion.
t: Timothy Cole: My Conversion From Pro-Life Speaker to Abolitionist
qt: I became increasingly frustrated and eventually got mad and shouted, “This presentation has nothing to do with ending abortion!”

q: How did Cole's pre-engagement phone call with Kevan Myers turn out?
a: Cole had planned to persuade Kevan that incremental bills were a path toward abolition, but Kevan walked him through scriptures on justice, impartiality, and the law as a tutor, and Cole ended the call as the one who had been persuaded.
t: Timothy Cole: My Conversion From Pro-Life Speaker to Abolitionist
qt: I’d planned to persuade and was the one being persuaded.

q: What scriptural framework finally convinced Cole that abolitionism was right?
a: Kevan walked him through passages on justice, refusing to show favoritism, and the function of the law as a tutor that teaches God's standards, leading Cole to see that scripture prescribes abolitionism in response to societal sin.
t: Timothy Cole: My Conversion From Pro-Life Speaker to Abolitionist
qt: Kevan walked me through scriptures about justice, not showing favoritism, and how the law is a tutor.

q: What concrete steps did Cole take after deciding to become an abolitionist?
a: He cancelled his Kansans For Life speaking engagement and called his pastor to alert him that his planned introduction of the Value Them Both amendment to the congregation would instead be a public argument against it.
t: Timothy Cole: My Conversion From Pro-Life Speaker to Abolitionist
qt: I cancelled my KFL speaking engagement and called my pastor to alert him that my introduction to the congregation of the Value Them Both amendment (the next morning) would be to speak against it.

q: How does Cole respond to the worry that turning pro-lifers into abolitionists leaves them with nothing to do about abortion?
a: He shared that concern at the time but has since found many opportunities to work toward abolition alongside Kevan's team in Kansas City and the folks at Abortion is Murder Kansas.
t: Timothy Cole: My Conversion From Pro-Life Speaker to Abolitionist
qt: I shared his concern at the time, but have since found many opportunities to work toward abolition in tandem with abolitionists in our area such as Kevan’s team in Kansas City, and the folks at [Abortion is Murder Kansas](https://aimks.org/).

q: What does Cole say pro-life legislation has actually taught Christians and the broader culture?
a: By protecting some babies and not others on arbitrary criteria, pro-life laws and rhetoric teach a warped view of justice, deceive secular culture, and poison believers' minds with what amounts to pro-abortion thinking.
t: Timothy Cole: My Conversion From Pro-Life Speaker to Abolitionist
qt: Pro-life efforts, with my support, have deceived the secular culture and poisoned the minds of believers with nothing less than pro-abortion thinking.

q: What lesson did Cole draw from sharing the Gospel with a father at Planned Parenthood?
a: Even after acknowledging the act was murder, the father chose to kill his baby for emotional reasons, an interaction that left Cole more passionately opposed to abortion and to any worldly philosophy that rejects the biblical mandate of immediate repentance.
t: Timothy Cole: My Conversion From Pro-Life Speaker to Abolitionist
qt: interactions like this which expose the evil of abortion in all its horror one make me more passionately despise abortion and any worldly philosophy that rejects the Biblical mandate of demanding our state and nation immediately repent for the sin of abortion.

q: How does Cole use Presbyterian abolitionist George Bourne to close his argument?
a: He cites Bourne's claim that the system of slavery admitted no cure but total and immediate abolition, since gradual emancipation is itself a recognition that the practice is rightful, drawing the parallel to abortion today.
t: Timothy Cole: My Conversion From Pro-Life Speaker to Abolitionist
qt: The system is so entirely corrupt that it admits of no cure but a TOTAL AND IMMEDIATE abolition.  For a gradual emancipation is a virtual recognition of the right, and establishes the rectitude of the practice.

q: Who was elected president of the Oklahoma Baptists at the 2020 Annual Meeting and who else was nominated?
a: Dr. Todd Fisher, pastor of Immanuel Baptist in Shawnee, was elected president; Leron West of Gilcrease Hills Baptist Church in Tulsa and Bill Ascol of Bethel Baptist Church in Owasso were the other nominees.
t: Todd Fisher Elected President of OK Baptists. A Step Toward or Away From Abolition?
qt: Dr. Todd Fisher, pastor of Immanuel Baptist in Shawnee, was selected by Oklahoma Baptist messengers at their 2020 Annual Meeting as the next president of the convention. He was nominated for the position by Oklahoma Baptists First Vice President Jeremy Freeman. Leron West of Gilcrease Hills Baptist Church in Tulsa (nominated by former Oklahoma Baptists President Bob Green) and Bill Ascol of Bethel Baptist Church in Owasso (nominated by First Baptist Church of Beggs pastor Chris Gore) were the other nominees.

q: What does Free the States consider to be one of Todd Fisher's primary responsibilities as president of the Oklahoma Baptists?
a: To demand the passage of the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act, since that legislation is the only one consistent with the convention's own platform calling for an immediate end to abortion without exception or compromise.
t: Todd Fisher Elected President of OK Baptists. A Step Toward or Away From Abolition?
qt: As President, one of his responsibilities is to demand the passage of the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act, as this is the only legislation consistent with the Oklahoma Baptist platform which states, “[W]e call upon the Oklahoma State Legislature to enact legislation that calls for the immediate end of abortion without exception or compromise.”

q: Why does the article argue that Oklahoma Baptists' stance matters so much to ending abortion in the state?
a: Because the convention is one of the most influential organizations on social policy in Oklahoma; once its leaders genuinely fight for abolition, abortion will not last long.
t: Todd Fisher Elected President of OK Baptists. A Step Toward or Away From Abolition?
qt: Oklahoma Baptists is one of the most influential organizations on social policy in the state. When the convention leaders decide to really fight for the abolition of abortion, abortion’s days will be few.

q: How did former president Blake Gideon move the Oklahoma Baptists toward abolition?
a: After convention leadership initially opposed SB13 in 2019, Gideon corrected course, preached a sermon supporting anti-abortion immediatism, tweeted that SB13 should receive a committee hearing, and at the 2020 annual meeting publicly called on the state to abolish abortion immediately.
t: Todd Fisher Elected President of OK Baptists. A Step Toward or Away From Abolition?
qt: While convention leadership originally came out in opposition to The Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act (SB13) in 2019, Gideon began correcting course and got the convention moving in the right direction. In 2020, Gideon preached a sermon supporting anti-abortion immediatism and tweeted that SB13 should receive a committee hearing.

q: What did Blake Gideon say at the 2020 annual meeting about abortion?
a: He affirmed the previous year's resolution against abortion, called abortion murder and a barbarous act against preborn neighbors, called on legislators to end it immediately, and offered the gospel of forgiveness to women who had had abortions.
t: Todd Fisher Elected President of OK Baptists. A Step Toward or Away From Abolition?
qt: As President of Oklahoma Baptists, we uphold last year’s resolution against abortion. We believe abortion is murder. It’s a barbarous act against our preborn neighbors. And we still call on state legislators to end abortion immediately. We would like to say to persons who have had abortion that there is forgiveness of sin in Jesus Christ. When we trust Christ as our personal Lord and savior, he separates us from our sin as far as the East is from the West.

q: What criticism did Muskogee Baptist Pastor Brett Baggett level against Oklahoma Baptists' leadership?
a: Baggett pointed out that the leadership did not vocally support the only 2020 bill that would have ended abortion immediately without exception or compromise.
t: Todd Fisher Elected President of OK Baptists. A Step Toward or Away From Abolition?
qt: Did the leadership vocally support the only bill in 2020 that would have ‘ended abortion immediately without exception or compromise’? Sadly, no.

q: What human cost does the article project if the new Oklahoma Baptist leaders fail to push for abolition?
a: Roughly 10,000 preborn children are likely to be murdered in Oklahoma during the two-year term of the newly elected leaders.
t: Todd Fisher Elected President of OK Baptists. A Step Toward or Away From Abolition?
qt: If Fisher and the rest of Oklahoma Baptists’ newly-elected leaders fail to do so, more than 10,000 preborn children are likely to be murdered in Oklahoma during the two-year term that they will serve.

q: How does Free the States ground its political demand on Christian leaders biblically?
a: By appealing to Isaiah 1:16-17, which calls God's people to cease evil, learn to do good, seek justice, correct oppression, and plead the cause of the defenseless before the civil authorities.
t: Todd Fisher Elected President of OK Baptists. A Step Toward or Away From Abolition?
qt: Those who represent the political voice of Oklahoma Baptists (and every Christian denomination) must boldly declare to those in civil authority, “Wash yourselves; make yourselves clean; remove the evil of your deeds from before [God’s] eyes; cease to do evil, learn to do good; seek justice, correct oppression; bring justice to the fatherless, plead the widow’s cause” (Isaiah 1:16-17).

q: Why does the author object to Oklahoma Baptists promoting Rose Day and Abby Johnson?
a: Rose Day is described as a platform for incrementalist politicians and lobbyists to celebrate "pro-life victories" used as excuses not to abolish abortion, and Abby Johnson is criticized for rejecting both immediate abolition and the fundamentals of Christian salvation by grace alone through faith alone.
t: Todd Fisher Elected President of OK Baptists. A Step Toward or Away From Abolition?
qt: Rose Day is an event for the state’s most powerful incrementalist politicians and lobbyists to tout their unjust “pro-life victories” which are used as the excuse not to abolish abortion. As for Johnson, she rejects the convention’s principles on ending abortion immediately and, more importantly, the fundamentals of the Christian faith starting with salvation by grace alone through faith alone.

q: What direct exhortation does Free the States issue to Fisher and the other newly seated Oklahoma Baptist officers?
a: To bring the Gospel into conflict with the culture of death, demand the total and immediate abolition of abortion without exception or compromise, and refuse to let political leaders settle for anything less than equal protection of law for all image bearers in Oklahoma.
t: Todd Fisher Elected President of OK Baptists. A Step Toward or Away From Abolition?
qt: It is our plea and our exhortation that, with all the strength within you, you bring the Gospel into conflict with the culture of death, that you demand the total and immediate abolition of abortion without exception or compromise, and that you do not let our political leaders off the hook with anything less than establishing justice and providing the equal protection of law to all image bearers in Oklahoma.

q: Who nominated Tom Ascol for SBC president and what positions does Ascol currently hold?
a: Ascol pastors Grace Baptist Church in Cape Coral, FL and leads Founders Ministries. His nomination was put forward by a group of Southern Baptists that included SBC First Vice President Lee Brand, signaling a level of insider support behind the candidacy.
t: Tom Ascol Nominated For Southern Baptist Convention President
qt: Tom Ascol, Pastor of Grace Baptist Church in Cape Coral, FL and president of Founders Ministries, was nominated for President of the Southern Baptist Convention (SBC) on Tuesday by a group of Southern Baptist pastors and members including SBC First Vice President Lee Brand.

q: What issues has Founders Ministries been pressing inside the SBC, and where does abolition fit in?
a: Founders has primarily fought feminism and critical race theory inside the convention, and Ascol has more recently turned his voice toward abolition by publicly criticizing SBC leaders who tried to suppress the Resolution on Abolishing Abortion.
t: Tom Ascol Nominated For Southern Baptist Convention President
qt: Ascol and Founders have become a force within the SBC, primarily fighting against feminism and critical race theory which have been creeping into the convention. In recent months, Ascol has become more outspoken on the subject of abolition, criticizing SBC leaders who tried to squash the Southern Baptist Resolution on Abolishing Abortion and who are still trying to oppose it.

q: What is the Ascol family's prior connection to abolitionist work at the SBC Annual Meeting?
a: Tom Ascol's brother Bill Ascol led the floor effort at the June 2021 SBC Annual Meeting, motioning to bring the Resolution on Abolishing Abortion out of committee and serving as its primary spokesman during debate.
t: Tom Ascol Nominated For Southern Baptist Convention President
qt: Ascol's brother, Bill Ascol, led the abolitionist charge at the SBC Annual Meeting last June, motioning to bring the Resolution on Abolishing Abortion out of committee and being the primary spokesman on its behalf while it was debated on the floor.

q: How does Dusty Deevers frame the case for Ascol's candidacy?
a: Deevers, the primary author of the Southern Baptist Resolution on Abolishing Abortion, casts Ascol as the 'sola Scriptura candidate' whose 44-year track record shows he will measure every issue, including abortion, sexuality, and sexual abuse, against the Word of God rather than cultural or elite pressure.
t: Tom Ascol Nominated For Southern Baptist Convention President
qt: Without reservation, I support Dr. Tom Ascol for President of the Southern  Baptist Convention. Dr. Ascol is the sola Scriptura candidate. Whatever the issue, Tom will do what he's done for 44 years as a SBC pastor: point his finger to the Word of God—not the winds of culture or talking points of the elite—and say, 'By this standard alone!'

q: What complaint about the SBC's trajectory do Ascol's endorsers share?
a: Endorsers like Brett White argue the convention has drifted from its biblical roots toward pragmatism and needs a leader with a proven record of defending Scripture's authority and sufficiency to steer it back.
t: Tom Ascol Nominated For Southern Baptist Convention President
qt: Sadly the SBC has strayed from her biblical roots and has long adopted pragmatic methodologies instead of resting on the authority and sufficiency of Scripture. Tom Ascol has a proven track record of being a defender of the truth, and I believe he would have the courage to steer the SBC back to the Bible.

q: What practical change do abolitionist pastors hope an Ascol presidency will produce?
a: Brett Baggett of Ekklesia Muskogee says messengers need 'men who are not scared to say what God says in His Word and then follow that speaking up with actual doing,' urging fellow SBC messengers to vote for Ascol because he is that kind of man.
t: Tom Ascol Nominated For Southern Baptist Convention President
qt: I will be voting for Tom Ascol to be the new president of the SBC, and I encourage you to as well, fellow messengers. We need men who are not scared to say what God says in His Word and then follow that speaking up with actual doing. By the grace of God, I think Tom is that kind of man.

q: Beyond abortion, what other doctrinal fights does Ascol's record represent for endorsers?
a: David Van Bebber points to Ascol's consistent advocacy through Founders Ministries for the SBC to denounce Critical Race Theory and address broader doctrinal drift across the denomination, presenting that record as evidence of the conviction needed for the presidency.
t: Tom Ascol Nominated For Southern Baptist Convention President
qt: Dr. Tom Ascol has been a consistent voice in advocating a robust response from the SBC to denounce Critical Race Theory and to deal with doctrinal drift  across the denomination. His fortitude, conviction, and love for the SBC have been on display in Founders Ministries for years.

q: What is Greg Treat's role in opposing SB13, and how has he used his position to block it?
a: Treat, as Senate Pro Tem, has been the primary obstacle to the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act across two sessions, both publicly attacking SB13 in the media and using his power inside the chamber to keep the bill from being heard.
t: Contrary to Treat's Media Tour, SB13 is Not a Secession Bill
qt: Oklahoma State Senate Pro Tem Greg Treat has been the primary opposition to the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act over the past two legislative sessions. Since the Abolition Day rally last year, Treat has gone to the media to denounce SB13, authored bills designed to be substitutes for SB13, and instructed former Senator Jason Smalley and Senator Greg McCortney not to hear SB13.

q: What was Treat's 2019 line of attack against SB13, and why did abolitionists consider it hypocritical?
a: In 2019 Treat denounced nullification as villainous, but abolitionists pointed out that he himself sponsored or voted for nullification measures on marijuana decriminalization and EPA regulations, undermining his stated principle.
t: Contrary to Treat's Media Tour, SB13 is Not a Secession Bill
qt: In 2018, he voted in favor of putting the decriminalization of marijuana on the ballot, a violation of the federal Controlled Substances Act and the supreme court's decision in *Gonzales v. Raich*. In 2016, he not only voted in favor of but was the primary sponsor of SB1379 which nullified federal EPA regulations.

q: How did Treat reframe his opposition to SB13 in 2020, and where did he make these claims?
a: After being exposed on nullification, Treat shifted to characterizing SB13 as a quasi-secession bill in interviews with the Norman Transcript and The Oklahoman.
t: Contrary to Treat's Media Tour, SB13 is Not a Secession Bill
qt: To the *Norman Transcript* last week, Treat said "I still am of the mind that [SB13 is] fatally flawed, and I believe that in large part that's a bill to secede from the union more than it is to save lives."

q: Who actually backs SB13, and why does Silberman list them?
a: A coalition of educational organizations, two Baptist denominations, the state Republican Party platform, and thousands of grassroots Oklahomans support SB13, demonstrating that the motivation is love of preborn neighbors rather than a fringe secessionist agenda.
t: Contrary to Treat's Media Tour, SB13 is Not a Secession Bill
qt: Free the States, The Ekklesia of Oklahoma, and Oklahomans United for Life are three political-educational organizations which support SB13. Two Christian denominations, the Oklahoma Association of Free Will Baptists and Oklahoma Baptists (Southern Baptist Convention), both passed resolutions in support of SB13 in the past nine months. The Oklahoma State Republican Party amended their platform to call for abortion's total and immediate abolition last year as well.

q: What does SB13 actually say that Treat is calling secession, and how does the article characterize it?
a: Section 33 declares federal actions purporting to override the Act void under both state and U.S. Constitutions; Silberman argues this is straightforward nullification, no different in kind from the marijuana and EPA measures Treat himself supported.
t: Contrary to Treat's Media Tour, SB13 is Not a Secession Bill
qt: Section 33 establishes that "Any federal statute, regulation, executive order or court decision which purports to supersede, stay or overrule this Act is in violation of the Constitution of the State of Oklahoma and the Constitution of the United States of America and is therefore void." This is no more a call to secession than Treat's SB1379 or the decriminalization of medical marijuana were examples of secession.

q: Why does Silberman accuse Treat of slander rather than honest disagreement?
a: Silberman argues Treat is misrepresenting the motives of thousands of abolitionists, casting their love for the preborn as a cynical cover for a secessionist agenda, in order to dodge the courageous action required of him.
t: Contrary to Treat's Media Tour, SB13 is Not a Secession Bill
qt: Treat seems to want you to believe that the motivation of all the work being done by all these various people and organizations is a desire to secede from the union rather than love for our preborn neighbors, and that we merely pretend to love our preborn neighbors while using them as a political tool to accomplish our actual objective.

q: What does the article say about the broader pro-life Republican establishment's use of the abortion issue?
a: Silberman argues that the pro-life political establishment treats abortion as a means to win elections rather than an injustice to actually end, and accuses Treat of doing the same through ongoing disinformation about SB13.
t: Contrary to Treat's Media Tour, SB13 is Not a Secession Bill
qt: "The life issue" is a means to an end for the Republican pro-life establishment. They pretend like they're doing something about abortion to appease their constituents and get reelected but show no desire to actually put an end to the slaughter.

q: How does Free the States frame its own motives in response to Treat's accusations?
a: The organization frames its work as a single-minded pursuit of justice for the preborn done in the name of Jesus Christ, and pledges that slander will not deter it or its allied organizations and activists.
t: Contrary to Treat's Media Tour, SB13 is Not a Secession Bill
qt: But as for the accusations, not a single staff or board member of Free the States has any objective in mind other than establishing justice for those who are currently being murdered under the cover of Oklahoma State Law, and doing so in the name of and for the glory of Jesus Christ.

q: What was the final vote share that ousted Paul Scott in the SD43 Republican primary runoff?
a: Challenger Jessica Garvin defeated incumbent Paul Scott by receiving 51.56 percent of the vote in the runoff.
t: Two Important Notes On Jessica Garvin Unseating Paul Scott
qt: In the OK SD43 Republican primary runoff, incumbent Paul Scott fell to challenger Jessica Garvin who received 51.56 percent of the vote.

q: How did Paul Scott vote when SB13 came up for a procedural motion on March 11, 2020?
a: Scott abstained on the motion to table SB13, apparently trying to avoid putting his position on either side of the abolition bill on the record.
t: Two Important Notes On Jessica Garvin Unseating Paul Scott
qt: When the motions were announced, Scott abstained, appearing to not want his vote on record one way or the other.

q: What did Senate Floor Leader Kim David do before the SB13 motions were called?
a: David spent more than thirty minutes walking the Senate floor lobbying Republican colleagues to support her motion to table SB13 and keep abortion legal in Oklahoma for another year.
t: Two Important Notes On Jessica Garvin Unseating Paul Scott
qt: Prior to either motion being announced, David roamed the Senate floor for more than half-an-hour whipping up votes among the Republican caucus (of which she is the second-ranking member) for her motion to keep abortion legal.

q: What motivated Kaity Keith to enter the SD43 race against Scott?
a: Keith, a constituent of Scott's, was horrified that he refused to vote to bring SB13 out of committee and decided to run because she believed someone had to stand up for the abolition of abortion.
t: Two Important Notes On Jessica Garvin Unseating Paul Scott
qt: Watching the events closely was Scott's constituent Kaity Keith. Horrified by his refusal to vote to bring SB13 out of committee, she decided to throw her hat in the ring for the seat.

q: How close did Paul Scott come to avoiding a runoff in the June 30 primary?
a: Scott missed an outright majority by only 14 votes, finishing at 49.9 percent with 5,685 votes while Garvin and Keith trailed at 39.3 and 10.9 percent respectively.
t: Two Important Notes On Jessica Garvin Unseating Paul Scott
qt: In the primary on June 30th, Paul Scott finished in first with 49.9 percent (5,685 votes). Garvin and Keith trailed with 39.3 percent (4,474 votes) and 10.9 percent (1,237 votes) respectively. Scott was a mere 14 votes away from the majority necessary to win the nomination.

q: Why does the article argue that Scott's SB13 abstention is what cost him the seat?
a: Without Keith in the race, Free the States contends Scott would have picked up far more than 14 of her 1,237 votes and won outright on June 30; instead the abstention drove Keith to run, forced a runoff, and Scott ultimately lost it.
t: Two Important Notes On Jessica Garvin Unseating Paul Scott
qt: Had Keith not joined the race, Scott, beyond any shadow of a doubt, would have received more than 14 of the 1,237 votes for Keith and won the nomination on June 30th.

q: What position did Jessica Garvin take on The Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act?
a: On her returned questionnaire Garvin said she would support the bill, even though she also said she would back compromise abortion legislation; Free the States said she would author or co-author the bill.
t: Two Important Notes On Jessica Garvin Unseating Paul Scott
qt: While Garvin said that she would support compromises with abortion, she also said that she would support The Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act.

q: Why did Free the States endorse Kaity Keith instead of Jessica Garvin despite Garvin's stated support?
a: Free the States endorsed Keith because she was the only consistent abolitionist in the field, while still being grateful that Garvin returned a questionnaire and pledged to back the bill.
t: Two Important Notes On Jessica Garvin Unseating Paul Scott
qt: We endorsed Keith because she was the consistent abolitionist in the race, but we were nonetheless very appreciative to receive a questionnaire from Garvin and happy to learn that she would author or co-author the bill.

q: What did Free the States plan to do with Garvin once she took office?
a: They intended to keep persuading her that compromise legislation sustains abortion and that only immediate abolition is faithful, while still working with her on the abolition bill she said she would support.
t: Two Important Notes On Jessica Garvin Unseating Paul Scott
qt: While we hope to persuade Garvin (as we hope to persuade everyone) that compromises with abortion are what is sustaining it, and that nothing short of the immediate abolition of abortion is required to be obedient to God, to uphold the Constitution, and to meaningfully love our preborn neighbors who are being murdered every single day that abortion isn't abolished, we are grateful for her expressed support for the bill and look forward to working with her along those lines.

q: How did Keith frame her decision to run on her own campaign website?
a: Keith said she ran because only four current state senators stood for human life in March 2020, arguing one cannot claim to favor life while voting for death.
t: Two Important Notes On Jessica Garvin Unseating Paul Scott
qt: I decided to run for state Senate when ONLY FOUR of our current Senators stood for human life this March. We cannot claim to be in favor of life, yet vote for death.

q: How did the abolitionist movement obtain Lauinger's internal strategy letter?
a: The letter was misaddressed by Lauinger's office and sent to the wrong senator, who then forwarded it to Free the States, exposing the procedural plan to kill SB13.
t: Uncovered Letter Exposes Plan to Keep Abortion Legal in Oklahoma
qt: A letter from Lauinger sent to the wrong Senator and then forwarded to the abolitionist organization Free the States details Lauinger's plan to prevent any consideration of abolishing abortion.

q: What specific procedural maneuver did Lauinger suggest to prevent SB13 from being attached to another bill?
a: Lauinger recommended moving SB614 from the Health and Human Services Committee, where SB13 author Sen. Joseph Silk sits, over to the Judiciary Committee where Silk has no seat and could not introduce an abolition amendment.
t: Uncovered Letter Exposes Plan to Keep Abortion Legal in Oklahoma
qt: "[SB614] could be moved from Health and Human Services to Judiciary, in which Sen. Silk does not sit," Lauinger wrote.

q: What parliamentary objections did Lauinger propose using against an abolition amendment?
a: He proposed ruling such an amendment out of order under the single-subject rule, arguing it was not germane because it touched many titles of law, or simply tabling any motion to amend.
t: Uncovered Letter Exposes Plan to Keep Abortion Legal in Oklahoma
qt: "An amendment regarding SB13 could be ruled out of order: as violating the single-subject rule (as it involves charges in many different titles of law); as being not germane (for the same reason – many different titles of law); [or] a motion to amend could be tabled. "

q: Why does Lauinger insist the legislature keep hearing other pro-life bills even while blocking SB13?
a: He argues that refusing to hear any abortion-related bills would freeze the pro-life movement's momentum, particularly the Rose Day bills already moving through the House.
t: Uncovered Letter Exposes Plan to Keep Abortion Legal in Oklahoma
qt: "Finally, Julie, refraining from hearing any abortion-related bills would paralyze the pro-life cause and make it impossible to make any progress. There were two other Rose Day abortion bills moving fast in the House."

q: Why does the article dismiss the sex-selective abortion ban (HB1396) as ineffective?
a: Because the law is trivially circumvented: a mother need only state any reason other than the baby's sex for the abortion, so no abortions are actually prevented.
t: Uncovered Letter Exposes Plan to Keep Abortion Legal in Oklahoma
qt: Bans on sex-selective abortions do nothing to prevent abortion. All the mother has to do is claim a reason for the abortion other than her baby's sex.

q: According to the author, what hidden purposes do these incremental pro-life bills serve?
a: They generate fundraising material for Oklahomans for Life, give Republican legislators talking points for pro-life voters, delay actual abolition so the pro-life industry retains power, and distract the public from the tabling of SB13.
t: Uncovered Letter Exposes Plan to Keep Abortion Legal in Oklahoma
qt: Delays the abolition of abortion so the pro-life industry and Republican establishment can maintain their political and financial power in perpetuity

q: What action does the article ask Oklahoma constituents to take in response to this revelation?
a: It urges readers to directly pressure their state representatives and senators to demand abolition rather than incremental measures, warning that without that pressure the killing will continue.
t: Uncovered Letter Exposes Plan to Keep Abortion Legal in Oklahoma
qt: Make sure your State Representatives and Senators know we demand abortion's abolition and nothing less. If we don't, the killing is not going to end.

q: What did the Utah-Idaho Southern Baptist Convention actually pass, and how contested was the vote?
a: The convention passed the Resolution on Abolishing Abortion (an adaptation of the 2021 national SBC resolution) with 79 cosigners, no substantive amendments from the Resolutions Committee, no opposition from any messenger on the floor, and a unanimous vote according to the resolution's drafter, Idaho pastor Paul Thompson. This stands in sharp contrast to other state conventions where similar resolutions have provoked floor fights between abolitionists and pro-lifers.
t: Utah/Idaho Baptist Convention Calls UT-ID Governors, Legislature to Abolish Abortion
qt: While abolitionist resolutions in other states have produced fierce debates on the floors of conventions between abolitionists and pro-lifers, the Utah-Idaho resolution sailed through without any substantial edits from the Resolutions Committee nor opposition from a single messenger.

q: Which specific officials does the resolution name, and what exactly does it ask them to do?
a: The resolution calls on Utah Governor Spencer Cox, Utah House Speaker Brad Wilson, Utah Senate President J. Stuart Adams, Idaho Governor Brad Little, Idaho House Speaker Scott Bedke, and Idaho Senate President Pro Tempore Chuck Winder, along with their respective legislatures, to pass, sign, and enforce legislation that would fully and immediately abolish abortion in their states without exception or compromise. It makes the same demand of President Joe Biden, Speaker Nancy Pelosi, and Senate President Pro Tempore Charles Schumer at the federal level.
t: Utah/Idaho Baptist Convention Calls UT-ID Governors, Legislature to Abolish Abortion
qt: Resolved, that as Southern Baptists we will engage, with God’s help, in establishing equal justice and protection for preborn children by calling upon Utah Governor Spencer Cox, Utah House Speaker Brad Wilson and the rest of the Utah State House, and Utah Senate President J. Stuart Adams and the rest of the Utah Senate to pass, sign, and enforce legislation that would fully, finally, and immediately abolish the heinous act of abortion in Utah immediately without exception or compromise

q: What scriptural and theological foundation does the resolution lay before getting to its political demands?
a: The resolution grounds its argument in the sufficiency of Scripture, the image of God in every human from fertilization, the equal worth of all humans regardless of circumstance, and God's establishment of civil authorities as His servants tasked with punishing evildoers. It explicitly cites the Baptist Faith and Message Article XV affirming life from conception and Southern Baptists' mandate to speak for the unborn.
t: Utah/Idaho Baptist Convention Calls UT-ID Governors, Legislature to Abolish Abortion
qt: Whereas, from the moment of fertilization, all humans are created in God’s image by, through, and for Jesus to the glory of God, and all souls belong to Him (Genesis 1:27; 4:1; 21;2 Isaiah 7:14; Colossians 1:16; Romans 11:36; Ezekiel 18:4)

q: What makes this resolution different from a typical pro-life church statement?
a: Silberman identifies the distinguishing feature as its naming of specific public officials in each state and the federal government and demanding they act immediately. Rather than deploring abortion in the abstract, it puts named governors, speakers, and senate leaders on the record as the ones being called to obedience, and rejects exceptions or compromise.
t: Utah/Idaho Baptist Convention Calls UT-ID Governors, Legislature to Abolish Abortion
qt: The distinctive feature of this resolution is its call for specific public officials in each state and the federal government to work to abolish abortion.

q: Why is the article so pointed about Idaho's existing abolition bill and its leadership?
a: Because Idaho already has a vehicle. State Representative Heather Scott has filed an abolition bill in each of the past three sessions, and Silberman charges that Governor Brad Little, House Speaker Scott Bedke, and Senate President Chuck Winder have done nothing to advance it. The Utah-Idaho SBC resolution gives those officials a religious constituency of 170 churches publicly demanding they stop stalling.
t: Utah/Idaho Baptist Convention Calls UT-ID Governors, Legislature to Abolish Abortion
qt: This is particularly applicable in Idaho where State Representative Heather Scott has filed an abolition bill each of the past three sessions. Governor Brad Little, House Speaker Scott Bedke, and Senate President Chuck Winder have not lifted a finger to support it thus far.

q: Does the resolution leave any room for the standard pro-life exceptions (rape, incest, life of the mother)?
a: No. It explicitly rejects any position that allows exceptions to what it calls the Holy and Righteous standard of Justice, asserting that no human institution has permission from God to allow the intentional shedding of innocent blood. This is a deliberate break from the exception-laden bills typical of mainstream pro-life legislation.
t: Utah/Idaho Baptist Convention Calls UT-ID Governors, Legislature to Abolish Abortion
qt: Resolved, that we, the messengers of the Utah/Idaho Southern Baptist Convention meeting in Meridian, Idaho October 29, 2021, agree with God that no human institution, government, rule, or authority has permission from God to allow the intentional shedding of innocent blood and we reject any position that allows for any exceptions to the Holy and Righteous standard of Justice

q: How does Scott Herndon situate this resolution within the longer trajectory of Idaho abolitionism?
a: Herndon, the director of Abolish Abortion Idaho and author of the state's abolition bill, traces a five-year arc: a 2016 abolitionist ballot initiative, the first abolition bill in 2019, an abolitionist Republican platform in 2020, and the 2021 SBC national resolution. The Utah-Idaho convention vote is presented as the next link in that chain, escalating from grassroots pressure to formal ecclesial demand on named officials.
t: Utah/Idaho Baptist Convention Calls UT-ID Governors, Legislature to Abolish Abortion
qt: He also tracked the progress of the movement in Idaho from an abolitionist ballot initiative in 2016, to the first abolition bill in 2019, to the making the Republican platform abolitionist in 2020, to the SBC victory in 2021.

q: What indictment does Herndon level against 48 years of conventional pro-life legislative strategy in Idaho?
a: Herndon argues the traditional pro-life approach of capitulating to court precedent has produced zero abortions banned in Idaho while tens of thousands of children have died. He frames the human cost in immediate terms (29 children per week) to argue that further delay by Bedke and Winder is itself culpable inaction, not prudence.
t: Utah/Idaho Baptist Convention Calls UT-ID Governors, Legislature to Abolish Abortion
qt: 29 children a week die while we tarry, and 48 years of traditional pro-life legislative efforts that capitulate to unconstitutional court tyranny have only resulted in NO abortions banned and tens of thousands of dead Idaho babies.

q: How does the resolution address the role of pastors and churches, not just civil officials?
a: Beyond the demands on government, the resolution charges pastors and churches to use preaching, teaching, and community leadership to direct people toward loving God and their preborn neighbor. It then closes with a pledge that the messengers themselves stand ready to act in rescue, citing Proverbs 24:11.
t: Utah/Idaho Baptist Convention Calls UT-ID Governors, Legislature to Abolish Abortion
qt: Resolved that we encourage pastors and churches to use their God-ordained gifts of preaching, teaching, and leading our communities to love the Lord our God with all our heart, soul, mind, and strength, and to love our neighbor — yes, even our pre-born neighbor — as if he/she were our own life

q: What does the resolution claim was already established by the 2021 national SBC meeting?
a: The Utah-Idaho resolution treats the 2021 national SBC's Resolution on Abolishing Abortion as already settled denominational policy, asserting that the messengers there declared abortion must be immediately abolished and that preborn children must receive equal protection of the law under both Scripture and the U.S. Constitution. Utah-Idaho is positioned as enforcing and applying that earlier vote at the state level.
t: Utah/Idaho Baptist Convention Calls UT-ID Governors, Legislature to Abolish Abortion
qt: Whereas, the messengers of the 2021 Southern Baptist Convention Annual Meeting affirmed and declared, the Southern Baptist Resolution on Abolishing Abortion, that abortion must be immediately abolished and preborn children must be afforded the equal protection of the law in accordance with God’s Word and the U.S. Constitution

q: What were the final vote percentages in the Senate District 7 general election?
a: Warren Hamilton won with 72.17 percent of the vote, while Democrat Jerry Donathan received 27.83 percent.
t: Warren Hamilton Wins!
qt: The Democrat in the Senate District 7 race Jerry Donathan garnered only 27.83 percent of the vote while Hamilton received a stunningly large 72.17 percent.

q: Why does Free the States consider Warren Hamilton a trustworthy legislator for the abolitionist cause?
a: Hamilton and his wife Sherrie have been active in the Abolitionist Movement since 2018, and the article presents him as someone who can be trusted to author and fight for a bill of total and immediate abolition.
t: Warren Hamilton Wins!
qt: He is someone the movement can trust to author a bill of total and immediate abolition and fight valiantly for its passage.

q: How did the defeated Republican incumbent Larry Boggs respond after losing the primary?
a: Boggs, who did not support immediate abolition, threw his support to the pro-abortion Democrat Jerry Donathan after losing the primary to Hamilton.
t: Warren Hamilton Wins!
qt: After being defeated, [Boggs threw his support to Donathan](https://www.facebook.com/DonathanForSenate/posts/178538857177436), the pro-abortion Democrat.

q: What did Holly Gerard claim about electability when she backed Boggs in the primary?
a: Gerard told Hamilton campaign volunteer Kyle Brown that she supported Boggs because he was the only Republican who could win the general election, an assessment Hamilton's landslide subsequently disproved.
t: Warren Hamilton Wins!
qt: She told Hamilton campaign volunteer Kyle Brown that she supported Boggs because he was the only Republican who could win the general election.

q: By how much did Hamilton outperform Boggs's 2016 general election numbers?
a: Hamilton outperformed Boggs's 2016 general election totals by more than 16 points; Boggs had only managed 55.75 percent against the Democrat in 2016.
t: Warren Hamilton Wins!
qt: Gerard's understanding of Oklahoma politics was proven embarrassingly wrong by Hamilton's victory as he outperformed Boggs' 2016 general election totals by more than 16 points.

q: What was the central issue that defined the Republican primary between Hamilton and Boggs?
a: The defining debate of the race was whether abortion should be immediately abolished, with Boggs opposing immediate abolition and Hamilton championing it.
t: Warren Hamilton Wins!
qt: Boggs did not support the abolition of abortion, and whether abortion should be immediately abolished was the defining debate of the race.

q: What does Free the States plan to do legislatively as a result of Hamilton's win?
a: The organization plans to introduce a bill to abolish abortion in Oklahoma the following year and is preparing to mobilize Oklahomans to support its passage.
t: Warren Hamilton Wins!
qt: There will be a bill to abolish abortion in Oklahoma next year and we are gearing up to rally the people of Oklahoma to support it.

q: How does the article characterize the political significance of Hamilton's victory beyond a single Senate seat?
a: It frames the victory as a massive win for the Abolitionist Movement and a simultaneous defeat for both anti-abolitionist Democrats and anti-abolitionist Republicans across Oklahoma.
t: Warren Hamilton Wins!
qt: Hamilton's election to the Oklahoma State Senate is a massive victory for the Abolitionist Movement and a massive defeat for anti-abolitionist Democrats and Republicans in Oklahoma.

q: What specific case did Bradley Pierce make at the Abolition Now Conference?
a: Pierce systematically walked through the seven planks of the mainstream pro-life political strategy and showed that, after nearly five decades, this combined approach has produced essentially nothing in the way of substantive progress against abortion.
t: WATCH: How the Seven-Pronged Pro-Life Political Strategy Has Failed
qt: Bradley Pierce, a constitutional attorney and director of Abolish Abortion Texas, presented on the seven aspects of the incremental pro-life strategy and how this seven-pronged strategy has made little to no progress in 47 years.

q: Hasn't the GOP-Supreme-Court pipeline been blocked by Democratic appointees?
a: No. Republicans have controlled the Supreme Court for almost the entire Roe era, and the very justices who issued and reaffirmed Roe were replaced by Republican-appointed justices, undercutting the premise that more Republican presidents will fix the court.
t: WATCH: How the Seven-Pronged Pro-Life Political Strategy Has Failed
qt: except for the few months between Scalia’s death and Gorsuch’s appointment, Republicans have held a majority of the supreme court for the entire 47 years since Roe was decided

q: What does Planned Parenthood v. Casey reveal about the Republican-judges strategy?
a: Casey shows the strategy can deliver a court packed with Republican appointees and still produce a full reaffirmation of legal abortion, demolishing the assumption that party affiliation predicts how a justice will rule on Roe.
t: WATCH: How the Seven-Pronged Pro-Life Political Strategy Has Failed
qt: in 1992, a supreme court made up of eight Republican appointees and a pro-life democrat upheld legal abortion in full in the *Planned Parenthood v. Casey* opinion

q: Among the current justices, who actually opposes Roe on the merits?
a: Only Clarence Thomas has clearly stated fundamental opposition to Roe in writing, and the other Republican appointees, including Alito and Roberts, have declined to join him, suggesting the supposed pro-life majority on the court is illusory.
t: WATCH: How the Seven-Pronged Pro-Life Political Strategy Has Failed
qt: Of the five justices on the current supreme court, only Clarence Thomas has ever indicated that he was fundamentally opposed to the *Roe* decision.

q: Why is Thomas reportedly voting against hearing abortion cases?
a: Pierce reads Thomas's reluctance as a signal that Thomas himself believes at least one of his Republican-appointed colleagues would side with Roe if a case were granted, making any new ruling more likely to entrench than dismantle the precedent.
t: WATCH: How the Seven-Pronged Pro-Life Political Strategy Has Failed
qt: Thomas knows that one or more of his Republican-appointed colleagues supports *Roe v. Wade*.

q: Aren't incremental laws at least chipping away at Roe in the courts?
a: No. Of the 37 pro-life laws that have actually reached the Supreme Court since 1973, every single one has been turned back, with Roe upheld in full each time and under Republican-majority courts at that.
t: WATCH: How the Seven-Pronged Pro-Life Political Strategy Has Failed
qt: Hundreds of pro-life laws have been passed since 1973, and 37 of them have gone before the supreme court. Yet not one iota of the *Roe* decision has been rolled back.

q: Even if Roe stands, aren't incremental laws still saving babies by lowering the abortion rate?
a: The data does not support that claim. Heavily regulated pro-life states are not seeing significantly steeper declines than pro-abortion states, and in some comparisons states with the most permissive abortion laws are dropping faster than the most regulated ones.
t: WATCH: How the Seven-Pronged Pro-Life Political Strategy Has Failed
qt: the abortion rate is actually dropping faster in New York than it is in Texas. That’s despite Texas having numerous incremental pro-life regulations and New York having the most pro-abortion laws in the country.

q: What does the author say Christians should do in response?
a: He calls for repudiating the incrementalist posture as unbiblical and timid, and instead openly defying Roe by demanding the immediate, total abolition of abortion rather than waiting for the court's permission.
t: WATCH: How the Seven-Pronged Pro-Life Political Strategy Has Failed
qt: We must put an end to the unBiblical, obsequious, timid pro-life strategy of incrementalism. We’ve got to stand up to the supreme court and the pro-life establishment to demand abortion’s immediate abolition in defiance of the horrifically evil and openly unconstitutionally *Roe v. Wade* opinion.

q: Why does the article urge readers to share the underlying video so widely?
a: Because persuading people to consider an alternative strategy first requires convincing them the dominant one is genuinely failing, and Pierce's empirical walk-through of court appointments and case outcomes is meant to do exactly that.
t: WATCH: How the Seven-Pronged Pro-Life Political Strategy Has Failed
qt: To understand why a different strategy is needed, it’s helpful for people to know that the currently strategy isn’t working.

q: Who is Joseph Silk and why does Free the States consider him a hero of the abolitionist movement?
a: Joseph Silk is an Oklahoma State Senator who carries the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act. Free the States holds him up as a hero because he has continued championing abolition legislation despite being punished by Senate leadership and the broader pro-life establishment for doing so.
t: Watch - Of Giants & Heroes: Joseph Silk
qt: Like us at Free the States, Apologia recognizes how fortunate the Abolitionist Movement is to have a Senator like Joseph Silk to carry the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act. Senator Silk has been punished greatly by Senate leadership and the pro-life establishment for standing up for preborn humans, yet hasn’t wavered.

q: What was the occasion that brought Apologia Studios to Oklahoma City to film Senator Silk?
a: Apologia Studios traveled to Oklahoma City to participate in the Abolition Now Conference and the Abolition Day rally, and used that visit to produce a video featuring Senator Silk.
t: Watch - Of Giants & Heroes: Joseph Silk
qt: Apologia Studios came to Oklahoma City to take part in the Abolition Now Conference and Abolition Day rally.

q: How does Apologia Studios characterize Joseph Silk in the promoted video?
a: Apologia frames Silk as a 'modern day hero of the faith' and 'the hero of Oklahoma,' presenting his life as a 'beautiful and inspiring story' meant to motivate viewers to spread the word about him.
t: Watch - Of Giants & Heroes: Joseph Silk
qt: Watch this powerful video about the hero of Oklahoma! Joseph Silk is a giant and a hero. This is his story. It is such a beautiful and inspiring story about a modern day hero of the faith.

q: What concrete actions does Free the States ask readers to take after watching the video?
a: The post directs readers to three actions: signing up for email updates because social media reach is being suppressed, becoming an activist using outreach materials like the 'Are You An Abolitionist' pamphlet from the Free the States store, and becoming a monthly donor to fund the movement.
t: Watch - Of Giants & Heroes: Joseph Silk
qt: Big tech is cracking down on our ability to reach you! Our social media reach has been greatly decreased in recent months. To make sure you are not missing important content and news about abolition bills from Free the States, [sign up for email updates]

q: What is the typical use of Romans 13 that this article seeks to invert?
a: Romans 13 is most often wielded as a one-way leash on Christians, demanding their submission to civil rulers, while the corresponding obligation that those same rulers must answer to God is left unpreached.
t: WATCH: Romans 13 Teaches That Civil Government Must Submit to Christ
qt: Romans 13 is commonly used to teach Christians about our duty to obey civil authorities. But rarely, if ever, is this chapter used to teach what the Lord reveals concerning the duty of those authorities themselves.

q: On what basis does Burkey argue that civil rulers are accountable to God rather than autonomous?
a: Because all authority is delegated by God, civil magistrates are God's servants in the civil sphere, which means they are not free agents and must study and obey His Word or face His judgment.
t: WATCH: Romans 13 Teaches That Civil Government Must Submit to Christ
qt: All governing authorities, being delegated and appointed by God, are *His servants* and are accountable to *Him*. As such, they have a duty to study and obey God’s Word — or face the consequences.

q: What classical Protestant voice does the article enlist to rebuke modern legislators?
a: She quotes the 17th century Puritan Richard Baxter, who told the rulers of his day that civil law must be subordinate to God's law and that no man can be a competent legislator without competent theology.
t: WATCH: Romans 13 Teaches That Civil Government Must Submit to Christ
qt: Remember that all your laws are to subservient to the laws of God, and never contrary to them, nor independent of them… A man can be a good theologian that is no [legislator], but he can be no good [legislator] that understandeth not theology.

q: What punishment does Jesus warn unfaithful overseers to expect, and how does Burkey apply that warning to politicians?
a: Jesus warns that servants entrusted with authority over others who abuse those in their care will be cut to pieces, and Burkey asks why corrupt politicians are never confronted with that very threat.
t: WATCH: Romans 13 Teaches That Civil Government Must Submit to Christ
qt: When is the last time a corrupt, ungodly politician was told to submit to Christ lest Christ cut him into pieces?

q: Who, according to Burkey, gets to define good and evil for the civil magistrate?
a: Because rulers are servants and not masters, they have no authority to define morality themselves; the master, God, defines good and evil and hands those definitions down through His Word.
t: WATCH: Romans 13 Teaches That Civil Government Must Submit to Christ
qt: (Good and evil are, of course, defined by the master and not the servant.)

q: How does the article quantify the failure of American civil authorities by their own God-given mandate?
a: She points to over sixty million children sacrificed nationally, nearly a million added every year, and 5,000 murdered annually even in the supposedly pro-life state of Oklahoma, all under the watch of magistrates God instituted to uphold justice.
t: WATCH: Romans 13 Teaches That Civil Government Must Submit to Christ
qt: More than sixty million children have been sacrificed in our country. Almost a million image bearers are added to that total nationally every year, and 5,000 are murdered annually in pro-life Oklahoma.

q: Whose responsibility is it to confront civil rulers with their duty before God?
a: Burkey lays the burden squarely on the church, framing the question rhetorically so that no other institution can plausibly fill the role the bride of Christ is abdicating.
t: WATCH: Romans 13 Teaches That Civil Government Must Submit to Christ
qt: Who is going to remind our civil authorities of their duty before God if the bride of Christ will not?

q: What concrete commitments does the article ask Christians to resolve to make?
a: Christians are to learn what God commands of magistrates, speak Scripture to them, hold them accountable, lovingly warn them, repent of their own apathy toward abortion, and speak up for the pre-born by faith.
t: WATCH: Romans 13 Teaches That Civil Government Must Submit to Christ
qt: Hold Godʼs civil servants accountable to God’s commands

q: What does Burkey say a Christian must repent of if they have remained silent toward unfaithful magistrates?
a: Believers must confess and repent before God of any participation in or apathy toward the abortion holocaust that has been allowed to continue because the church refused to hold civil servants accountable.
t: WATCH: Romans 13 Teaches That Civil Government Must Submit to Christ
qt: Confess to God and repent of any participation in or apathy toward the abortion holocaust that we have allowed to take place by not holding Godʼs civil servants accountable

q: What two competing abortion-related legislative priorities were before the Texas GOP convention committee, and who was behind each?
a: The committee considered immediate abolition versus the 'Texas Abolition Strategy,' an incremental package put forward by Texas Right to Life that bundled a PRENDA bill, a heartbeat bill, and a partial abolition bill.
t: WATCH: Wesley Thomas' Brilliant Testimony at the TX GOP State Convention
qt: The first was abortion's immediate abolition. The other was the "Texas Abolition Strategy," an incremental, Roe-submissive plan put forward by Texas Right to Life centered around a PRENDA bill, a heartbeat bill, and a partial abolition bill.

q: How lopsided was grassroots testimony at the committee hearing between abolitionists and incrementalists?
a: Testifiers favoring immediate abolition outnumbered the incrementalist witnesses by a five-to-one margin during the committee hearing.
t: WATCH: Wesley Thomas' Brilliant Testimony at the TX GOP State Convention
qt: Testifiers in favor of immediate abolition outnumbered incrementalist testimonies five-to-one during the committee hearing.

q: How does Wesley Thomas reframe the charge that abolition bills 'criminalize women'?
a: Thomas rejects the framing entirely. He argues abolitionists are not pushing to criminalize a class of people but to make specific acts criminal, and that anyone who participates in homicide should be legally culpable.
t: WATCH: Wesley Thomas' Brilliant Testimony at the TX GOP State Convention
qt: What I would say is – number one – that we're not pushing for the criminalization of anyone. We're seeking to make criminal certain acts. Anyone involved with homicide should be culpable…

q: When pressed about a 17-year-old girl driven to an abortion facility by her mother, what principle does Thomas appeal to?
a: He appeals to consistency under existing homicide law: whatever the Texas penal code already does to a 17-year-old who kills her one-year-old, it should do for the same act against a preborn child. The standard is equal treatment, not new or harsher punishment.
t: WATCH: Wesley Thomas' Brilliant Testimony at the TX GOP State Convention
qt: If you have a 17-year-old mother who killed her one-year-old child, all we're saying – here's all we're saying – the law as it exists today in the penal code, how the law treats that one-year-old child, it should treat the preborn child exactly the same way.

q: What dual authority does the article cite to ground its demand for equal protection?
a: The piece grounds equal protection of the preborn in both Scripture and the U.S. Constitution, citing Proverbs 24:10 as a Biblical mandate and the 14th Amendment as a constitutional one.
t: WATCH: Wesley Thomas' Brilliant Testimony at the TX GOP State Convention
qt: we are simply demanding the [equal protection of the laws be applied to all human beings](https://freethestates.org/criminalization/index.html). That's a Biblical mandate (Proverbs 24:10) and a Constitutional mandate (14th amendment).

q: Why does the author single out Thomas' testimony for special attention rather than the other abolitionist testimonies?
a: Because of the live exchange Thomas had with incrementalist committee members—particularly Jonathan Gaspard's pointed questions about criminalizing women—which produced what the author calls a 'fantastic interaction' modeling how to answer the standard objection.
t: WATCH: Wesley Thomas' Brilliant Testimony at the TX GOP State Convention
qt: Wesley Thomas' testimony in particular is worth calling attention to here because of the exchanges he had with incrementalist members of the committee. One of the Committee Members, Jonathan Gaspard, asked Thomas about the criminalization of abortion and a fantastic interaction ensued.

q: How does Mr. P. describe the underlying motivation that led him and his wife to choose IVF despite his prior moral objections?
a: He frames it as a self-reliant attempt to complete what felt like a missing piece of their life, an effort to seize their own happiness rather than trust God with their childlessness.
t: Weeping Time Documentary: The Beginning of an IVF Conversation
qt: It was a very morally compromised decision to go forward with IVF, and the reason why we did is because we felt like we were missing something in our life. You look at it sort of like a puzzle, right? There was a piece of our puzzle that was empty, and if we don't have a child, our puzzle will never be complete.

q: What happened to the seven embryos the P. family created through IVF?
a: All seven died, and the couple was informed in a cold meeting at the fertility office four days after the procedure.
t: Weeping Time Documentary: The Beginning of an IVF Conversation
qt: They received a call four days later asking them to come to the fertility office for a meeting in which they were coldly told that *all* of their embryos had died.

q: How does Mr. P. distinguish his view of his lost embryos from how he believes many other Christians regard them?
a: He explicitly counts the embryos as his children whose deaths he grieved, contrasting himself with Christians who treat the embryo as something less.
t: Weeping Time Documentary: The Beginning of an IVF Conversation
qt: I don't have the problem that a lot of Christians do today where they look at the embryo as something other than their child. So for me, it was very emotional. It was basically — my children died,

q: What does the Weeping Time documentary cover, and who is featured in it?
a: It is a C.R. Cali production that interviews IVF families, embryo-adopting families, an IVF doctor, and theologians including Dusty Deevers, Blake Gideon, Kelly Greene, Owen Strachan, and George Grant.
t: Weeping Time Documentary: The Beginning of an IVF Conversation
qt: The project features interviews with families who have participated in traditional IVF, families who have adopted abandoned embryos, an IVF doctor, and theologians including Dusty Deevers, Blake Gideon, Kelly Greene, Owen Strachan, and George Grant.

q: How did the broader Christian ethics academy respond when Cali approached them about the IVF documentary?
a: Silberman notes that none of the numerous Christian ethics professors Cali contacted were willing to be interviewed on the subject.
t: Weeping Time Documentary: The Beginning of an IVF Conversation
qt: (While a number of pastors and theologians are featured, not one of the numerous Christian ethics professors Cali approached for an interview wanted anything to do with this subject matter.)

q: Why does Silberman argue abolitionists must not let attention to abortion eclipse the IVF issue?
a: Because the IVF industry is also waging a war on the image of God in human life, even while the Church is rightly turning more attention to abortion.
t: Weeping Time Documentary: The Beginning of an IVF Conversation
qt: Abortion is rightly beginning to receive the level of attention from the Church it has long deserved, but we must not overlook the war against the image of God being waged by the IVF industry and its proponents.

q: According to Dr. John David Gordon, why does standard IVF practice produce so many surplus embryos?
